BPD is pretty much /r/CPTSD. There's research saying that it's a chemically caused dysregulation of emotion from methylation of the body because you're trapped in chronic stress which leads to depression and anxiety.When I was at my worst, I was literally not in control as I was so confused and disoriented. I was a vessel for a tangled webs of extremely heightened emotions that I couldn't cope with, and everything was hair-trigger. My behaviour gradually became more and more extreme because I felt like I needed more validation (I never ever had any) and in doing that, my behaviour was increasingly normalised to me and I didn't realise the extent or impact of my behaviour until now, in hindsight.Talking to people who told me that there was something wrong with me was extremely triggering and sent me plunging into a spiral of self hatred and a loss of hope because I didn't know what was wrong with me at that time./u/onethecamden

Pour over coffees at home poured into a nice Zojirushi travel mug. Back when I used a Grind-n-Brew drip maker, the grinder wasn't very good and automatic drips provide lower-quality extraction. My cheap IKEA thermos also didn't hold heat terribly well, so I'd make coffee in the morning, drink about half of it and then go and get Starbucks later in the day. I dropped around $200 on equipment (well, I would have, but half of that came from gifts from my in-laws) and now I have a much better coffee routine with much higher quality brew than Starbucks usually makes. Similarly, I do a lot of handwriting and prefer to use fountain pens. My pens are pretty frugal (from $3-28) compared to Mount Blancs, and I've had the same two bottles of $10 ink for years. Overall, I'm coming out ahead compared to all of the Pilot G2 and Fisher Space Pen cartridges I've gone through in the years before that.

As Stroud said, Swiftcast is basically mandatory for higher content, and in general is really helpful after you hit level 28 (I think 28 is where you get the trait to allow in-combat resurrection).You'll also get Blizzard II along the way, which is more AoE than you'll have until Holy at 45.  Surecast as well, but surecast has...much more limited applications in general PvE content.  Thaumaturge to 26 WILL happen to you eventually if you're playing a caster or healer, simply for swiftcast.  The end :pAside from that, Arcanist has your other skills.  Virus comes up very early and is a great cross class for anyone who can cross it - though you'll want to take care to let scholars and summoners use it on bosses if you've got one, since theirs becomes traited to reduce magic damage eventually.  Yours will not.  This doesn't mater if a boss only does physical damage, though.Eye for an Eye is at 34 Arcanist and is useful as well.I don't remember exactly the order I have mine setup as.  I think it's Swiftcast, Blizzard II, Virus, Eye for an Eye, and Surecast last.  Blizzard II is only so early in the list so I can have it for most low level roulettes I'm in.

You don’t have to “invest heavily in a relationship” before determining sexual compatibility. I’m saying... don’t invest in a relationship at all before you know someone. My current dude and I met six months or so ago. We had some attraction but I wasn’t prepared to just start dating somebody I didn’t know well. I said no, and dated a friend of mine I’ve known for years instead in an intentional sort of rebound situation. I got to know the other dude and when he didn’t turn out to be a bad guy after six months, I started dating him when my rebound relationship ended. Waiting the six months first was a good thing: it revealed the things about the guy that I liked and the things I didn’t, and allowed me to make an informed decision about whether or not I wanted to have those things in my life. BEFORE I slept with the dude.I don’t know why people are always in such a hurry. Relationships are like good wine: a little age is never a bad thing.

I'm not. I'm not really trying to offering a defense of Trump, more a comparison between worrying about nuclear war, and worrying about Cruz implementing his ideals. Trumps staff picks not withstanding, Trump was also a bit unknown regarding implementing similar policies to Cruz. And I think it still is, simply because it's IMO clearly not a focus of his. He doesn't hold those ideals nearly as strongly as Cruz does.Personally, I'd say Trump cares about Christianity just about the same amount that he cares about LGBT rights - he's happy to say that he supports those causes, but they're not a focus and he'll dump them if it means getting something else he wants. It just happens that the people that fit what he wants are also generally anti-LGBT and pro-Christanity, so it 'works out' that way. That said, at the end of the day it hardly matters *why* he's pushing such ideals and legislation if he is - and his cabinet picks definitely indicate that he is.

INTJs lead with Ni while ISTJs with Si. For INTJs their main process is seeing interconnections through metaphors and symbolism (For example you might see a connection between snakes and pain so every time you'll be in pain you'll think of snakes or between green and money, etc.) and abstractly learning with diving deep unconsciously seeing the meaning of concepts and the hidden meaning in them and connections, some report that they just "stare" at something for long enough until they somehow understand it without having actual coherent thoughts or being able to explain how they got there.Some also report having random visions about the future, just "knowing" what is going to happen, like a gut feeling. Ni is future oriented, it is always planning for the future and the past is constantly repressed. "Things change through time" would be a motto for Ni users. As a result of always repressing the past, everything they think about the past is unconsciously repressed in the negative part of the subconscious, so they will only point out the mistakes they made (or others) in the past instead of the good things to. For the INTJ though, the future is filled with both good and bad things, after all INTJs plan everything.ISTJs main process is Si. Si is often described exactly as what nostalgia should be, it is seeing something in the present and comparing it to the past. While INTJs live in the future, ISTJs live in the past, the first thing they think of when they face a problem is if they ever dealt with this in the past. Generally xSxJ types will try out new things (with Ne) only until they find one that works. When they find something that works they will stick with it. ISxJ types are sometimes afraid of trying new things so they will learn EVERY SINGLE FUCKING POSSIBLE SITUATION POSSIBLE they might deal with in the future, memorizing them like prayers so they will never have to explore the unknown. ISTJs will try out only what worked in the past and if they haven't faced that situation in the past they might feel doomed so that's why they would try to always be prepared for all the negative things of the future.Si also deals with how the environment affects the user (Sensing wise). While INTJs might feel very detached from their body, ISTJs are always in tune with internal sensorial sensations like hunger, thirst, pain, temperature, etc. As a result of constantly thinking about the past, ISTJs will repress their past. While INTJs will always think of the past as negative, (the future being neutral), ISTJs will always think of the future as negative. "Only bad things will happen in the future so we must be prepared for everything!"While both are like "How does does everything I've ever experienced, felt, imagined and thought relate to this new piece of information/situation I'm in?", Ni is from past to present, while Si is from present to past. Ni is constantly thinking about the future sometimes going to the past to understand it. What ISTJs (Si) do is maybe see "alternative" histories, random things that could have happened but it happens rarely (inferior Ne), mostly they will think JUST about the past.EDIT: What I wrote in this comment should be enough for your typing but if you want to read a very in-detail description of Si vs. Ni, read the replies. Otherwise ignore it. :)

My relationship with my master relationship definitely has DD/lg elements but we've never felt the need to actually slap that label on there, and we've certainly never felt the need to do certain things to fit a certain label.  I somewhat consider myself a little but I don't actually pretend to be any younger.  I just have a lot of childlike qualities and I like a lot of things aimed at younger people.  My master's very caring and nurturing and daddy-like but I rarely call him that.  As for the pet thing, my master's pet name for me is "bunny", which has also become a nickname of mine, but I don't consider myself a pet or identify as a pet in any way.  It's just a word and it doesn't actually make me feel like an animal.  I'm not doing any animal roleplay or anything, despite being called that.  That in mind I *personally* don't think simply being called an animal name is petplay itself *but* if you want to consider yourself a pet because of your nickname, that's entirely your prerogative.  These things are what you make them.

Things can get reignited, yes. But OP's wife has never been comfortable having the type of sex with him that he wants from her, and he sees this as an obligation of sorts. *She did it with her ex, so she should do it with him.* Well, sexual desires don't work like that. The type of sex you want with one person may not work with someone else. This is definitely my experience. And coming at it from the perspective of, "You did it with him so you owe it to me" is not going to help. Perhaps he could have a conversation like, "l would like to do X in bed. Is there anything that could make you comfortable to try that?" But you will never guilt someone into wanting freaky sex.

So be specific. We have permits and gun safety education courses (again the people committing the crimes usually aren't doing this anyway). And those permits get renewed every year. So what's the proposal, self shooting guns?The thing is that whatever law we pass, it won't end violence or even make it that much harder for criminals to obtain and use weapons. Criminals don't care about the laws to begin with. It will however make it harder for a law abiding citizen to own a weapon for self defense or whatever other purpose they have. Do some of the research and you'll find that even though gun crime obviously went down in the UK after the 96 gun ban, violent crime rose and especially home invasion rates. Quick anecdote, I was in the Navy on a submarine and though modern submarines don't often go into battle they do serve a purpose known as "strategic deterrence." That's what guns are in the hands of the law abiding citizen, strategic deterrence against crime.

There's another element I'll add to this. Hunting doesn't bother me (as long as it's skillful and respectful, so not trophy hunting). I'll gladly eat venison. I recognize the time and effort which goes into tracking an animal. But...I still hate these pictures for a reason you didn't touch on. I'm trying to decide if in physically attracted to him. Ignoring the fact that he probably doesn't look his best at the end of a hunt, *I'm not sexually attracted to dead animals.*   (Not that I am to live ones lol, but cute puppy pics don't kill my attraction to a guy, they're just cute.) I'm trying to see if he'd turn me on, if I'm attracted to him, and a dead body or bloody snow just kills any arousal I may have felt. Given the difficulty faced by guys online already, it seems like a bad idea to use photos which negate attraction (assuming I'm not the only one who feels this way lol).
